name: abab6
tagline: MiniMax's first 100B+ parameter Mixture-of-Experts large language model, fully released in early 2024.
seoTitle: MiniMax abab6 — China's First 100B+ MoE LLM
metaDescription: MiniMax abab6, fully released in early 2024, was billed as China's first 100B+ parameter Mixture-of-Experts large language model, with a 32,768-token context l…
note: First 100B+ MoE LLM in China; ~32K context
overview: abab6 is MiniMax's Mixture-of-Experts large language model, fully released in early 2024 after a period of internal testing that followed a late-December 2023 preview. MiniMax described it as the first large language model in China with a parameter scale above one hundred billion built on a MoE architecture, and said the company developed its own efficient MoE training and inference framework to support it.

As an API chat model (abab6-chat), abab6 supported a combined input-plus-output token limit of 32,768 tokens. MiniMax compared it against GPT-4 and GPT-3.5 on instruction-following and alignment benchmarks such as IFEval, MT-Bench, and AlignBench, reporting that abab6 surpassed GPT-3.5 and Claude 2.1 on several capabilities while still trailing GPT-4 overall.

abab6 was a stepping stone between MiniMax's earlier abab5.5 generation and the trillion-parameter abab6.5 series that arrived in April 2024. It has since been retired in favor of newer MiniMax models, so this page documents it as part of the legacy abab line.
strengths: First large language model in China above 100B parameters using a MoE architecture
Built on MiniMax's own efficient MoE training and inference framework
Reported gains over GPT-3.5 and Claude 2.1 on several capabilities
32,768-token combined input/output context for its generation
useCases: General-purpose Chinese and multilingual chat assistants
Reasoning and writing within a 32K-token budget
API-based application development on the MiniMax platform

faq.q: What was notable about MiniMax abab6?
faq.a: MiniMax billed abab6 as the first large language model in China above one hundred billion parameters built on a Mixture-of-Experts architecture, supported by an in-house efficient MoE training and inference framework.
abab6 was previewed at the end of December 2023 and fully released in early 2024 after internal testing, ahead of the trillion-parameter abab6.5 series that followed in April 2024.
The abab6-chat API model supported a combined input-plus-output token limit of 32,768 tokens.

faq.q: How did abab6 compare to GPT-4?
faq.a: MiniMax evaluated abab6 on benchmarks including IFEval, MT-Bench, and AlignBench, reporting that it surpassed GPT-3.5 and Claude 2.1 on several capabilities while still trailing GPT-4 overall. MiniMax did not publish a complete set of exact scores.
